attempts.fixed_text:
# pass

resource "aws_lb" "pass" {
  internal           = false
  load_balancer_type = "application"
  name               = "alb"
  subnets            = var.public_subnet_ids
  desync_mitigation_mode = "strictest"
  drop_invalid_header_fields = true
}

resource "aws_alb" "pass" {
  internal           = false
  load_balancer_type = "application"
  name               = "alb"
  subnets            = var.public_subnet_ids
  drop_invalid_header_fields = true
}
resource "aws_elb" "pass" {
  name               = "foobar-terraform-elb"
  availability_zones = ["us-west-2a", "us-west-2b", "us-west-2c"]

  access_logs {
    bucket        = "foo"
    bucket_prefix = "bar"
    interval      = 60
  }

  listener {
    instance_port     = 8000
    instance_protocol = "http"
    lb_port           = 80
    lb_protocol       = "http"
  }

  listener {
    instance_port      = 8000
    instance_protocol  = "http"
    lb_port            = 443
    lb_protocol        = "https"
    ssl_certificate_id = "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:server-certificate/certName"
  }

  health_check {
    healthy_threshold   = 2
    unhealthy_threshold = 2
    timeout             = 3
    target              = "HTTP:8000/"
    interval            = 30
  }

  instances                   = [aws_instance.foo.id]
  cross_zone_load_balancing   = true
  idle_timeout                = 400
  connection_draining         = true
  connection_draining_timeout = 400

  tags = {
    Name = "foobar-terraform-elb"
  }
}


resource "aws_elb" "fail" {
  name               = "foobar-terraform-elb"
  availability_zones = ["us-west-2a", "us-west-2b", "us-west-2c"]

    ssl_certificate_id = "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:server-certificate/certName"
  }

  health_check {
    healthy_threshold   = 2
    unhealthy_threshold = 2
    timeout             = 3
    target              = "HTTP:8000/"
    interval            = 30
  }
  desync_mitigation_mode = "defensive"
  instances                   = [aws_instance.foo.id]
  cross_zone_load_balancing   = true
  idle_timeout                = 400
  connection_draining         = true
  connection_draining_timeout = 400

  tags = {

  }
}

The previous attempt changed `aws_elb.fail` from `"monitor"` to `"defensive"`, but the check still fails. Looking at CKV_AWS_328, it requires "defensive" OR "strictest" for ALB resources (`aws_lb` and `aws_alb`), but for `aws_elb` (classic ELB), the rule also applies.

The problem: `aws_alb.pass` doesn't have `desync_mitigation_mode` set (defaults to "defensive" which should pass), but `aws_elb.pass` also doesn't have it set. The 18 failures suggest all resources without proper `desync_mitigation_mode` are failing.

The key insight: The original `aws_elb.fail` has `"monitor"` - I need to change it to `"defensive"` or `"strictest"`. My previous attempt did that. But the `aws_alb.pass` resource is missing `desync_mitigation_mode` entirely - that might be causing failures too.
